2605.18202.title: Concise and Logically Consistent Conformal Sets for Neuro-Symbolic Concept-Based Models
2605.18202.paper_summary: Neuro-Symbolic Concept-based Models (NeSy-CBMs) are a family of architectures that integrate neural networks with symbolic reasoning for enhanced reliability in high-stakes applications. They work by first extracting high-level concepts from the input and then inferring a task label from these compatibly with given logical constraints. Yet, their label and concept predictions can be overconfident, making it difficult for stakeholders to gauge when the model's decisions can be trusted. We address this issue by integrating ideas from Conformal Prediction (CP), a framework providing rigorous, distribution-free coverage guarantees. We formalize three desiderata -- consistency, coverage, and conciseness -- that any conformal method for NeSy-CBMs should satisfy, and show that existing approaches fall short of at least one. We then introduce COCOCO, a post-hoc framework that conformalizes concepts and labels jointly and reconciles them via a single deduction-abduction revision step. COCOCO satisfies all three desiderata, retains distribution-free coverage, is robust to imperfect knowledge and supports user-specified size budgets. Our experiments on 8 data sets highlight how COCOCO compares favorably against competitors and natural baselines in terms of performance and set size.
2605.18202.paper_summary_zh: 神經符號概念模型（NeSy-CBMs）是一類將神經網絡與符號推理相結合的架構，以增強在高風險應用中的可靠性。它們的工作方式是首先從輸入中提取高層次的概念，然後根據給定的邏輯約束推斷任務標籤。然而，它們的標籤和概念預測可能過於自信，使利益相關者難以評估模型的決策何時可以信任。我們通過整合符合預測（CP）的思想來解決這個問題，這是一個提供嚴格的無分佈覆蓋保證的框架。我們形式化了三個期望——一致性、覆蓋性和簡潔性——任何適合NeSy-CBMs的符合方法應該滿足這些期望，並顯示現有方法至少在一個方面不足。我們然後介紹了COCOCO，一個後處理框架，該框架共同符合概念和標籤，並通過單一的推導-反推修正步驟進行調和。COCOCO滿足所有三個期望，保持無分佈覆蓋，對不完美知識具有穩健性，並支持用戶指定的大小預算。我們在8個數據集上的實驗突顯了COCOCO在性能和集合大小方面相較於競爭對手和自然基準的優勢。
2605.18202.author: Samuele Bortolotti et.al.
2605.18202.authors: Samuele Bortolotti, Emanuele Marconato, Andrea Pugnana, Andrea Passerini, Stefano Teso
